resources.title: Analog / Digital Oscilloscope
resources.subtitle: Waveform capture for analog and digital circuit analysis
resources.description: Multi-channel oscilloscopes for observing and measuring voltage waveforms in analog and digital circuits built in the CIL.
resources.overview: The Circuits & Innovation Lab provides both analog and digital storage oscilloscopes at student workstations. These are used throughout the semester for verifying circuit waveforms, measuring RC time constants, characterising op-amp frequency response, and debugging microcontroller outputs.
applications: RC and RL circuit time-constant measurement

resources.title: Function / Signal Generator
resources.subtitle: Sine, square, and triangle waveform generation
resources.description: Programmable function generators used to provide test signals for analog circuit experiments and frequency response analysis.
resources.overview: The function generators in CIL allow students to generate precise sine, square, sawtooth, and triangle waveforms as inputs to circuits under test. They are frequently used for op-amp characterisation, filter design experiments, and PWM generation for motor control experiments.

resources.title: Arduino / Microcontroller Kits
resources.subtitle: Embedded systems and interfacing platform
resources.description: Arduino Uno and Mega development boards used for microcontroller-based projects involving sensors, actuators, and digital interfaces.
resources.overview: The CIL maintains a set of Arduino Uno and Arduino Mega boards used for microcontroller interfacing experiments. Students use these to implement digital I/O, ADC sampling, PWM motor control, I2C and SPI sensor interfacing, and serial communication. These boards are available for issue from the component counter.
applications: Motor and servo control via PWM

resources.title: DC Regulated Power Supply
resources.subtitle: Stable dual-channel supply for circuit experiments
resources.description: Adjustable dual-channel DC power supplies for powering analog, digital, and microcontroller circuit experiments.
resources.overview: CIL workstations are equipped with dual-channel regulated DC power supplies. These support both positive and negative supply rails, making them suitable for op-amp experiments requiring split supplies. They are also used for powering logic circuits, microcontroller breadboard prototypes, and motor driver modules.

specs.val: Dual LED V and A readout

resources.title: Digital Multimeter
resources.subtitle: Portable DMM for in-circuit measurements
resources.description: Handheld digital multimeters for voltage, current, resistance, and diode measurements at every student bench.
resources.overview: Each workstation in the CIL has a handheld digital multimeter for quick in-circuit measurements. These are used to verify supply voltages, measure component values before assembly, check logic levels at microcontroller pins, and troubleshoot open circuits or shorts on breadboard prototypes.

resources.title: Soldering Stations
resources.subtitle: Temperature-controlled soldering for PCB assembly
resources.description: Temperature-controlled soldering stations for PCB fabrication and component soldering in project-based lab work.
resources.overview: The CIL has a set of temperature-controlled soldering stations available for student project work. These are used for soldering through-hole and surface-mount components onto PCBs, repairing prototype boards, and assembling custom circuit modules for course projects and research work.

projects.title: Touchless Tap
projects.description: A cost-effective product developed to ensure safety during the Corona pandemic. The sensor-based water tap does not require manual operation and works when it detects your hand under the nozzle. Designed and developed by Research Engineers Sana & Khagendra.

projects.title: ELEO Chatbot (v1)
projects.description: The first version of ELEO (ECE Lab Enquiry Officer) was a simple rule-based, keyword-matching chatbot created by B.Tech student Jayan Pahuja under the guidance of Engineers Sana Ali Naqvi & Khagendra Joshi. It has since been completely replaced by the current AI-powered assistant.

projects.title: ELEO AI Assistant (v2)
projects.description: The current ELEO chatbot is a fully AI-powered assistant developed by Rahul Pardasani (M.Tech CSE 2025). It uses LLMs, a RAG pipeline, and vector search to answer lab queries dynamically.
